messages.body_plain:
Andres, Lori,


Everything looks fine for gDNAx on the last report of the release and 
the devel builder, except for a TIMEOUT while checking examples in the 
devel build of taishan (aarch64), but which doesn't seem to be related 
to the ExperimentHub cache problem, so hopefully at some point it will 
go away by itself.


Thanks for your help!


robert.

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Bioconductor packages and upcoming changes to ggplot2
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Bioconductor packages and upcoming changes to ggplot2
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone,

We've been preparing a new major version of ggplot2 and expect that some
packages might be broken.
If you do not maintain a package that depends on ggplot2, you can safely
skip reading this.

Unfortunately, our own reverse dependency testing infrastructure based on
the revdepcheck package has some issues checking Bioconductor packages.
We're not confident we can fix the infrastructure within a short timeframe,
which is why I'm reaching out to you in this aspecific manner.
If you maintain a package that uses ggplot2, we're asking if you can test
your package against the new version.
You can test your code with the development version of ggplot2 by
installing it as follows:

```r
# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("tidyverse/ggplot2")
```

If you find any problems, either with ggplot2 or perhaps in the interaction
with your package, please do not hesitate to reach out. You can file a
possible bug report at https://github.com/tidyverse/ggplot2/issues or tag
@teunbrand on GitHub if you want me to take a look. Below you can find a
list of common issues and possible solutions, where you're also welcome to
raise discussion:

* https://github.com/tidyverse/ggplot2/issues/6498
* https://github.com/tidyverse/ggplot2/issues/6504
* https://github.com/tidyverse/ggplot2/issues/6505
* https://github.com/tidyverse/ggplot2/issues/6507
* https://github.com/tidyverse/ggplot2/issues/6515

I'm happy to assist in the run-up to the release, but I'd need help finding
the places where assistance is needed. We aim to release the new ggplot2
version in about 2 weeks. Hopefully this will give you notice in a timely
manner.

Best wishes,
Teun

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Reminder: EuroBioC2025 Late-Breaking Poster Deadline – Friday, July 11
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Reminder: EuroBioC2025 Late-Breaking Poster Deadline – Friday, July 11
messages.body_plain:
Just a quick reminder that the late-breaking poster submission deadline for EuroBioC2025 is this Friday, July 11.

Submit here: https://forms.gle/ewTGdKzB2xyEg58f8

We look forward to seeing your abstracts!

Maria Doyle, PhD
Bioconductor Community Manager

From: Maria.Doyle <Maria.Doyle at ul.ie>
Date: Friday, 20 June 2025 at 20:53
To: bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Subject: EuroBioC2025: Late-Breaking Poster Call Now Open
Dear Bioconductor community,

We are now accepting late-breaking poster abstracts for EuroBioC2025, taking place in Barcelona, September 17?19, 2025.

If you missed the initial submission round, this is a great opportunity to present your work and connect with the Bioconductor community.


  *   Submission deadline: Friday, July 11, 2025
  *   Submission form: https://forms.gle/ewTGdKzB2xyEg58f8
  *   Notification of acceptance: by July 15
  *   Optional short poster pitch during the conference

Topics of interest include (but are not limited to):


  *   Genomics, transcriptomics, proteomics
  *   Multiomics and data integration
  *   Infrastructure, visualisation, benchmarking
  *   Statistical methods and developer tools

More information: https://eurobioc2025.bioconductor.org

Please feel free to share with colleagues or collaborators who may be interested.

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Join BioHackathon Europe 2025 + R & Rust Developer Forum (28 July)
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Join BioHackathon Europe 2025 + R & Rust Developer Forum (28 July)
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor developers,

We have two developer-focused opportunities to share with the community:

1. BioHackathon Europe 2025 ? Join Our Project

A Bioconductor project was selected for BioHackathon Europe 2025 (https://biohackathon-europe.org/) (Berlin, Nov 3-7), and we?d love to involve the wider developer community.

In-person application closes Monday, July 15, but virtual participation remains open.


  *   Bioconductor project details<https://github.com/elixir-europe/biohackathon-projects-2025/blob/main/13.md>
  *   All projects<https://biohackathon-europe.org/projects/>

If you've questions, please reach out or join us on Bioconductor Zulip<https://chat.bioconductor.org/> #edam-collaboration channel.

2. R + Rust in Bioinformatics ? Online Developer Forum

We?ve just published a blog post announcing an upcoming online session exploring how R and Rust can work together in bioinformatics and Bioconductor development.


  *   Date: Monday, 28 July 2025
  *   Time: 17:00?18:00 CEST
  *   Location: Online
  *   Details & agenda: Read the blog post https://blog.bioconductor.org/posts/2025-07-09-developers-forum-july28/

Organised by Llu?s Revilla, this session will feature talks on extendr, Rust-based package development, and real-world use cases. All are welcome.

Thanks, and hope to see some of you at these events.

Best regards,
Maria

Maria Doyle, PhD

Hi,

Best,
Nirav Malani

>
> Please take the time to address this by committing and pushing
> changes to your package at git.bioconductor.org
>
> Notes:
>
> * This was the status of your package at the time this email was sent to you.
> Given that the online report is updated daily (in normal conditions) you
> could see something different when you visit the URL(s) above, especially if
> you do so several days after you received this email.
>
> * It is possible that the problems reported in this report are false positives,
> either because another package (from CRAN or Bioconductor) breaks your
> package (if yours depends on it) or because of a Build System problem.
> If this is the case, then you can ignore this email.
>
> * Please check the report again 24h after you've committed your changes to the
> package and make sure that all the problems have gone.
>

> maintenance of your package, please use the Bioc-devel mailing list:
>

>

>
> Thanks for contributing to the Bioconductor project!
>

messages.body_plain:
Hi
Thank you for this informative e-mail. However, I am at a loss for an answer.
Compilation errors only appear on merida1 (old version of OS X) and nebbiolo1 machines. On kjohnson1 and Palomino7, there are no BUILD nor CHECK problems.

Furthermore, when I look at the CHECK logs, it turns out that the errors come from the reticulate package, which I only import into the Pirat package; it's therefore difficult for me to understand and correct the problem.

How can I prevent the Pirat package from being placed in deprecation?
Thank you for your help

Sincerely

Samuel Wieczorek

De : CoreTeam Bioconductor <bioconductorcoreteam at gmail.com<mailto:bioconductorcoreteam at gmail.com>>
Envoy? : lundi 7 juillet 2025 19:05
? : WIECZOREK Samuel <samuel.wieczorek at cea.fr<mailto:samuel.wieczorek at cea.fr>>
Objet : Bioconductor package Pirat

Hello Package Maintainer,

We would like to bring to your attention that your package is failing in both release and devel Bioconductor on all platforms. This is very problematic. Please fix the package in the next few weeks to avoid deprecation.

https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/release/bioc-LATEST/
https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/devel/bioc-LATEST/

If you have further questions or concerns please reach out on the bioc-devel at r-project.org<mailto:bioc-devel at r-project.org>

We appreciate your quick attention to this matter

Cheers,
On behalf of the Bioconductor Core Team

messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] chemmineob build failure due to missing zip lib
messages.body_plain:
These errors look like they're actually coming from the OpenBabel library
itself (libopenbabel.a), rather than your package code.  That library is
looking for symbols defined in zlibbioc e.g. bioc_deflateEnd and it's not
finding them now you're linking against the regular zlib.  I think this
means someone needs to recompile the OpenBabel library installed on the
build machine so that it also uses the regular zlib, and then your changes
will be accurate.

Mike

looking for symbols defined in zlibbioc e.g. bioc_def

Thanks Mike, that sounds like the likely cause.

Could someone from bioc please re-compile OpenBabel on palomino8 using 
the "z" library instead of "zlibbioc". Thank you.

Kevin
